commit.message:
refactor: replace scopes table reads with org_cache lookups (5b-4) (#4453)

* refactor: replace scopes table reads with org_cache lookups (5b-4)

Eliminate all scopes table read operations from the request resolution
path by using org_cache lookups backed by Clerk API.

Core changes:
- resolveScope() uses getOrgData/getOrgBySlug instead of scopes table queries
- getDefaultScope() uses org_cache with JWT fast path and Clerk API fallback
- resolveOrgId() replaces resolveScopeId() returning orgId directly
- ResolvedScope type: { orgId, slug, tier } replaces full scopes DB record
- ResolvedMember type: { role, userId } removes scopeId field
- AuthContext/RunnerAuthContext: add orgId field from CLI tokens
- RuntimeScope: remove id field, keep { slug, orgId }
- CreateRunParams: remove scopeId field, use orgId exclusively
- getScopeById made private, getScopesByOrgIds removed (unused)
- ensureDefaultScope always returns full DB record for write paths
- setScopeTier test helper updates org_cache alongside scopes table
- setupUser pre-populates org_cache for default Clerk org

Updates ~35 route files to use orgId instead of scopeId in auth context
and service function calls. Net -92 lines.

Closes #4447

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use real org creation date in scope members api

fetch organization created-at timestamp from Clerk API inside
getScopeMembers() instead of accepting a placeholder new Date()
parameter, ensuring the scope members endpoint returns the real
organization creation timestamp

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor(web): remove defensive try-catch in runner group token generation (#4448)

Let Ably token generation errors propagate naturally instead of
catching and returning null. This follows the "avoid defensive
programming" principle — callers can now distinguish between "Ably not
configured" (null return) and "token generation failed" (exception).

The route handler's createHandler error handler already provides a
generic 500 response for unhandled exceptions.

Closes #4434

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: stop populating scope_id in INSERT operations (5b-3) (#4427)

* refactor: stop populating scope id in insert operations (5b-3)

Remove scopeId from .values({...}) in all production INSERT operations
across 8 dependent tables (agent_runs, agent_composes, agent_schedules,
connectors, model_providers, secrets, storages, variables) and test
helpers. Since scope_id columns are already nullable (5b-1), new rows
will have scope_id = NULL with no functional impact.

Also removes the now-unused scopeId parameter from ensureStorageExists()
and cleans up the scopeId variable in enqueueRun() that was only used
for the INSERT.

Closes #4425

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: update test assertion and remove stale jsdoc for scope id removal

Update create-run test to no longer assert scopeId value since it is no
longer populated in INSERT. Remove stale @param scopeId JSDoc from
ensureStorageExists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update cascade-scope-deletion tests to reflect scope_id no longer populated

Since Phase 5b-3 stops populating scope_id in INSERT operations,
agent_composes records have null scope_id and are no longer
cascade-deleted when a scope is deleted. Update test assertions
to expect records to be retained instead of deleted.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: sanitize api error responses to prevent information leakage (#4426)

* fix: sanitize api error responses to prevent information leakage

Add centralized createSafeErrorHandler to ts-rest-handler.ts that returns
generic error messages to clients while logging full details server-side.
Replace 6 duplicate per-route errorHandler functions, sanitize env var
names, OAuth callback errors, Slack OAuth errors, and proxy error messages.
Addresses CWE-209 and OWASP ASVS V7.4.1.

Closes #4390

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle path parameter validation errors in safe error handler

The createSafeErrorHandler only handled bodyError and queryError from
ts-rest validation, missing pathParamsError. This caused path parameter
validation failures (like invalid connector type) to return 500 instead
of 400. Also update tests to match sanitized error messages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: rename clerk identity fields to provider-agnostic names (#4412)

* refactor: rename clerk identity fields to provider-agnostic names

Unify naming convention by removing the `clerk` prefix from identity
fields throughout the codebase. Clerk's own SDK already uses `orgId`
and `userId`, making the prefix redundant.

Changes:
- Rename DB column `clerk_org_id` to `org_id` across 12 tables
- Rename 16 indexes (idx_*_clerk_org* to idx_*_org*)
- Update all Drizzle schema definitions
- Rename clerkOrgId to orgId in ~95 TypeScript files
- Rename clerkUserId to userId parameter names in scope services
- Rename functions: getScopeByClerkOrgId to getScopeByOrgId, etc.
- Update test helpers, test files, and documentation

Closes #4405

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve ci failures in rename refactor

- Fix TypeScript error: restore scopeId as `string | null` in
  refreshConnectorToken, upsertConnectorSecret, and upsertSecretByScope
  (type was incorrectly narrowed from `string | null` to `string` during
  the rename, but agentRuns.scopeId is nullable in the schema)
- Fix migration consistency: add missing PK index rename for
  org_members_cache (clerk_org_id_user_id_pk -> org_id_user_id_pk)
- Fix format: auto-format 0129_snapshot.json with Prettier

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add security response headers for casa compliance (#4403)

* fix: add security response headers for casa compliance

Add 5 ASVS V14.4 required security headers to turbo/apps/web via
Next.js headers() config (CASA-APP-002):

- X-Frame-Options: DENY
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
- Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
- Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains
- Content-Security-Policy: permissive default-src with restricted
  script-src whitelist and frame-ancestors 'none'

Phase 1 uses permissive CSP (unsafe-inline for script-src) to avoid
breaking third-party SDKs (Clerk, Sentry, Plausible, Termly).

Closes #4389

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add clerk cdn and cloudflare domains to csp, add style-src directive

The e2e-auth test failed because Clerk's inline styles were blocked by
CSP (no style-src fell back to default-src * which excludes unsafe-inline)
and Clerk CDN scripts from *.clerk.com were not whitelisted in script-src.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use correct api() helper for explorium service config

The merge with main introduced explorium using the renamed service() helper
that was reverted. Use the existing api() helper to match the rest of the file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove duplicate service config block from connectors.ts

The service proxy config was extracted to services.ts in main but the
PR branch still contained the original block, causing lint, type-check,
and knip failures due to unused code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use permissive csp script-src to avoid breaking clerk auth

The restrictive script-src was blocking Clerk's authentication flow in
preview deployments, causing e2e-auth CI failures. Use wildcard script-src
matching the permissive default-src approach for phase 1, with plan to
tighten in a future iteration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add user_cache table for caching clerk user data (#4377)

* feat: add user_cache table for caching clerk user data

Add a DB-backed read-through cache for Clerk user data (user_cache table)
following the same pattern as org_cache. This caches the two most
frequently called uncached Clerk APIs:

- getUserEmail (20+ call sites) now reads from user_cache with 1-min TTL
- getUserIdByEmail (2 call sites) uses reverse email lookup on user_cache

Part of #4371 — unifying all Clerk reads behind JWT → Cache → API pattern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: clear user_cache between tests to prevent cross-test contamination

Tests reuse hardcoded emails (e.g., user@example.com) across test cases
with different user IDs. The user_cache table persists across tests,
causing stale lookups that return wrong user IDs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove user_cache full-table delete to prevent test race condition

The afterEach hook was performing a full-table delete on user_cache.
When test files run in parallel workers sharing the same database,
this caused a race condition: one worker's cleanup would delete cache
rows that another worker's test just inserted, causing intermittent
assertion failures in user-cache-service.test.ts.

Since each test uses a unique userId via uniqueId(), data is already
naturally isolated and the full-table delete is unnecessary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: eliminate test race condition in user-cache-service tests

The user_cache table is shared across parallel vitest workers. The
afterEach full-table DELETE (needed to prevent cross-test contamination
from shared emails) could race with cache writes in another worker,
causing intermittent "expected null not to be null" failures.

Instead of querying the database to verify cache rows exist (which is
vulnerable to concurrent DELETE), verify caching behavior by calling the
function twice and asserting the second call does not hit Clerk API.
This tests the same behavior without the race condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: format migration metadata files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
refactor: switch forward scope.slug reads to org_cache via getOrgData (#4373)

* refactor: switch forward scope slug reads to org cache via getorgdata

migrate 10 production locations from direct scopes.slug DB reads to
getOrgData(clerkOrgId).slug, using the org_cache table as the source
of truth for forward (clerkOrgId → slug) lookups.

Production changes:
- schedule-service: replace getScopeSlug(scopeId) with getOrgData(clerkOrgId)
- build-context: replace scopes query fallback with getScopeById + getOrgData
- execution-preparer: remove scopes JOIN, use getOrgData post-query
- permission-service: remove scopes JOIN from getEmailSharedAgents
- slack/telegram/github integration routes: remove scopes JOIN

Test infrastructure:
- Pre-populate org_cache in createTestScope, test helpers, and GitHub helpers
- Add insertOrgCacheEntry with onConflictDoUpdate for idempotency
- Add deleteOrgCacheEntry and disableAllSchedules test helpers
- Fix org-cache-service tests for cache-miss/stale scenarios

Closes #4361 (PR1 of 2)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: switch reverse scope slug reads to org cache via getorgbyslug

Add getOrgBySlug() for reverse lookups (slug → clerkOrgId) using
org_cache with Clerk API fallback. Add slug index on org_cache table.
Update Clerk mock to support { slug } parameter for getOrganization.

Migrate 7 production locations from direct scopes table queries or
getScopeBySlug to getOrgBySlug:
- email/handlers/shared.ts (resolveAgentByAddress)
- agent-compose/resolve-default.ts (resolveDefaultAgentComposeId)
- platform/logs/route.ts (scope filter + display)
- integrations/slack/route.ts (target scope resolution)
- integrations/telegram/route.ts (target scope resolution)
- integrations/github/route.ts (target scope resolution)
- agent/composes/route.ts (cross-scope lookup)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: migrate resolve-scope and admin tier route to use org cache

Switch resolve-scope.ts and admin/scope/tier/route.ts from direct
scopes table queries (getScopeBySlug) to org_cache lookups via
getOrgBySlug. Un-export getScopeBySlug since it is now only used
internally within scope-service.ts. Add getOrganization mock to
setupClerkOrgMock and pass orgSlug in scope test helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct migration journal timestamp ordering for 0126

Migration 0126 had a timestamp earlier than 0125, violating the
journal ordering constraint checked by CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: merge compose job cleanup into per-minute cleanup-sandboxes cron (#4354)

* fix: merge compose job cleanup into per-minute cleanup-sandboxes cron

Compose jobs could get stuck in pending/running state indefinitely when
the webhook callback from E2B sandbox failed, blocking users from
creating new jobs due to the partial unique index. The standalone
cleanup-compose-jobs cron only ran once daily with a 24h timeout.

- Add compose job stale detection to cleanup-sandboxes cron (10min timeout)
- Delete standalone cleanup-compose-jobs cron and its daily schedule
- Reduces user-blocking time from 24 hours to ~11 minutes

Closes #4352

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: sync contract schema and add compose job error tracking

Update cleanupResponseSchema to include composeJobsCleaned and
composeJobErrors fields, and add reason to cleanupResultSchema.
Track compose job cleanup errors in the response for observability
parity with sandbox cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use direct db insertion for cleanup-sandboxes tests

After the e2b executor removal refactor, createTestRun dispatches runs
which fail on org_members_cache lookup, setting status to "failed".
Cleanup cron only finds pending/running runs, so tests couldn't verify
cleanup behavior. Switch to createTestRunInDb which inserts directly
into the database with the desired status.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: auto-format cleanup-sandboxes route

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: replace scope_members with clerk api in permission check (#4343)

* feat: replace scope_members with clerk api in permission check

Replace the scope_members + scopes table JOIN in canAccessCompose()
with Clerk-based membership verification. Uses JWT fast path when the
active org matches (zero API calls), falls back to Clerk Backend API
for cross-org or non-session contexts (e.g. email webhooks).

Closes #4336

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use user-scoped membership query to avoid pagination risk

Query memberships by userId instead of organizationId to avoid
pagination issues with large organizations where legitimate members
could be denied access if they fall outside the first page of results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add clerk api fallback path coverage for org membership check

Add test case that exercises the Clerk API fallback path when the
user's active org differs from the compose's org but the user is
still a member of the compose's org.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add org_members_cache for clerk api membership checks

Use org_members_cache as a read-through cache for Clerk API membership
lookups in canAccessCompose(). Cache hit avoids Clerk API call entirely;
cache miss fetches from Clerk API and upserts the cache row. TTL is
1 minute, aligned with Clerk JWT TTL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make cache write fire-and-forget and add cache layer tests

Cache upsert failure no longer blocks access after Clerk API confirms
membership. Added integration tests for cache hit, stale cache, and
stale-cache-with-non-member paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add pagination limit and void prefix for cache write

Set limit: 100 on getOrganizationMembershipList to handle users in
many orgs. Add void prefix to fire-and-forget cache write to satisfy
no-floating-promises lint rule.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(mitm-addon): stream all responses to prevent zlib error (#4350)

* fix(mitm-addon): stream all responses to prevent zlib error

mitmproxy buffers and re-encodes gzip responses by default, causing
ZlibError on chunked gzip responses (e.g., api.anthropic.com). Since
the response() hook only reads metadata and never processes body
content, it is safe to stream all responses unconditionally.

Also adds [build-system] to pyproject.toml and updates CI to install
test dependencies via `pip install ".[test]"` instead of hardcoding
package names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mitm-addon): use content-length header for response size in logs

Since streaming is now enabled for all responses, flow.response.content
is empty. Use the content-length header instead to report response_size
in network logs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(mitm-addon): assert response_size in latency log test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(mitm-addon): fix stale mock setup in response handler tests

Replace MagicMock().content with explicit headers dict so tests
properly exercise the content-length header code path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent zero meet skills list flash and spurious auth reloads (#4338)

* fix: prevent zero meet skills list flash and spurious auth reloads

Stop Clerk listener from triggering reload$ on token refreshes by
tracking the previous user ID and only reloading on actual sign-in /
sign-out changes. Add optimistic connected state for connectors so the
skills list transitions directly from "Connecting…" to "Connected"
without flashing. Cache previous loadable data so the list stays visible
during refetch cycles. Improve button labels for API-key-only connectors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve restricted react import lint error in zero-meet-page

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: align api-token routing with button label logic in zero-meet-page

The onConnect handler used `.includes("api-token")` to route connectors
to the settings dialog, while the button label used `length === 1 &&
[0] === "api-token"` to show "Add API key". This meant dual-auth
connectors (OAuth + API-token) would be routed to the settings dialog
but display "Connect" as their label. Aligned both conditions to check
for api-token-only connectors.

Also added a clarifying comment on the render-body side effect for
clearing optimistic state, explaining why it is self-limiting and safe.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add regression tests for auth reload filtering and optimistic connector state

- Auth tests verify reload$ only fires on user ID change (sign-in/out),
  not on token refreshes
- Connector tests verify optimistic connected state is set after api
  token submit and cleared via command
- Updated mock-auth to support firing clerk listeners for auth tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: switch user preferences read path to clerk jwt claims (#4344)

* feat: switch user preferences read path to clerk jwt claims

Add JWT fast path to getUserPreferences() that reads from Clerk
membership session claims (membership_timezone, membership_notify_email,
membership_notify_slack) when available. Falls back to scope_members DB
read for non-JWT contexts (cron, run-builder, CLI tokens).

- Add optional sessionClaims param to getUserPreferences() and
  setTimezoneIfNotSet()
- Update API route to pass sessionClaims from auth()
- Update verifyDeviceAction to pass sessionClaims
- Add integration tests for JWT claims path, DB fallback, and partial
  claims
- Extend Clerk mock with membership claim options

Closes #4335

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: switch preferences fallback from scope_members to clerk api

Replace scope_members DB fallback with Clerk API
getOrganizationMembershipList() to read membership publicMetadata.
This eliminates getUserPreferences() dependency on
getPrimaryAdminMembership() and scope_members table.

- Change signature to getUserPreferences(clerkOrgId, userId, claims?)
- Clerk API fallback reads publicMetadata (timezone, notify_email,
  notify_slack) from membership
- Update all callers to pass clerkOrgId:
  - API route: from auth().orgId or scopeId lookup
  - build-context: from runtimeClerkOrgId
  - schedule-service: from scopeRecord.clerkOrgId
  - cli-auth: from auth().orgId
- Simplify setTimezoneIfNotSet dual-write using clerkOrgId param
- Update Clerk mock to return publicMetadata on memberships
- Add Clerk API fallback integration test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address pr review findings for clerk preferences

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add org_members_cache for clerk api rate limiting

Add a DB-backed read-through cache (org_members_cache) for Clerk
membership preferences, following the org_cache pattern. This prevents
Clerk API 429 rate limit errors in hot paths (runs, schedules) by
caching membership data with a 1-minute TTL aligned with JWT TTL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct migration journal timestamp ordering

Migration 0125 had a timestamp earlier than 0124, causing the
test-migration-consistency check to fail. Updated to maintain
monotonically increasing timestamps.

commit.message:
refactor: remove legacy credential concept entirely (#4345)

* refactor: remove legacy credential concept entirely

Remove all remnants of the "credential" concept which was the original
name for what is now called "secrets". This includes:

- Delete credential API routes and their 410 stubs
- Delete credential contract definition from @vm0/core
- Remove credential methods from CLI API client
- Remove credentials from variable expander (regex, types, interfaces)
- Remove credential-to-secrets aliasing/fallback logic
- Rename crypto functions: encryptCredentialValue → encryptSecretValue,
  decryptCredentialValue → decryptSecretValue
- Rename internal variables in build-context.ts from credential* to
  secret*/platformSecrets terminology
- Remove credentials from source-derivation, schedule-utils, and
  agent status display
- Update ~35 consumer files for crypto function renames
- Update all tests to reflect removal

Closes #4341

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address code review findings

- Remove remaining `grouped.credentials` references in slack, github,
  and telegram integration routes
- Fix schedule setup test assertions that referenced removed
  `result.credentials` property
- Update agent status test to use `secrets.GITHUB_APP` instead of
  `credentials.GITHUB_APP`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: drop extra argument from expand-environment test calls

The credential parameter was removed from expandEnvironmentFromCompose
but the test file still passed an extra undefined argument, causing
TS2554 (wrong argument count) and runtime TypeError (boolean not iterable).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove credential references from e2e agent status tests

Remove test for credentials display and update mixed test to only
check secrets and vars, consistent with credential concept removal.

commit.message:
feat: remove Clerk org creation from scope service (#4240)

* feat: remove clerk org creation and require clerk org id in scope creation

Clerk now auto-creates organizations for new users, so vm0 no longer
needs to call createOrganization(). This makes clerkOrgId a required
parameter in createScope() and updates POST /api/scope to resolve
clerkOrgId from the user's Clerk org memberships before creating a scope.

Closes #4234

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: optimize scope query to filter by user org ids instead of loading all scopes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add early return for empty clerk org memberships to avoid empty inarray query

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: extract clerk org resolution into shared service function

Move Clerk org resolution logic from route handler into a shared service
function, keeping the route handler thin and eliminating duplication with
discoverAndCreateScope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pass clerk org id to test scope creation fallback

The createScope() signature now requires clerkOrgId. Update the
ensureTestScope fallback to pass a sentinel test clerkOrgId.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove clerk mock reset in storage webhook tests

The storage webhook routes call getDefaultScopeByClerkUserId which
resolves scope via Clerk API memberships. Setting mockClerk({ userId: null })
after setupUser caused the Clerk client mock to lose the user's org
memberships, making scope resolution fail with "User's default scope
not found". Keeping the original mock from setupUser preserves the
membership data needed for scope resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update tests to use correct clerk org id after removing org creation

Tests that derive clerkOrgId from scope slug now either use the actual
clerkOrgId from the scope record or set up explicit clerkOrgs before
creating scopes so the POST route resolves the expected org ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: remove clerk org creation and require clerk org id in scope creation

Clerk now auto-creates organizations for new users, so vm0 no longer
needs to call createOrganization(). This makes clerkOrgId a required
parameter in createScope() and updates POST /api/scope to resolve
clerkOrgId from the user's Clerk org memberships before creating a scope.

Closes #4234

* fix: optimize scope query to filter by user org ids instead of loading all scopes

Move Clerk org resolution logic from route handler into a shared service
function, keeping the route handler thin and eliminating duplication with
discoverAndCreateScope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pass clerk org id to test scope creation fallback

The createScope() signature now requires clerkOrgId. Update the
ensureTestScope fallback to pass a sentinel test clerkOrgId.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove clerk mock reset in storage webhook tests

The storage webhook routes call getDefaultScopeByClerkUserId which
resolves scope via Clerk API memberships. Setting mockClerk({ userId: null })
after setupUser caused the Clerk client mock to lose the user's org
memberships, making scope resolution fail with "User's default scope
not found". Keeping the original mock from setupUser preserves the
membership data needed for scope resolution.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: update tests to use correct clerk org id after removing org creation

Tests that derive clerkOrgId from scope slug now either use the actual
clerkOrgId from the scope record or set up explicit clerkOrgs before
creating scopes so the POST route resolves the expected org ID.

commit.message:
feat(platform): wire zero meet settings tab to real api (#4192)

* feat(platform): wire zero meet settings tab to real api

Extract ZeroSettingsTab as a sub-component and wire agent name save to
real compose API. When the user changes the agent name and clicks Save:
- Fetches the current compose content via zeroOnboardingStatus$
- Updates the agents key in the compose YAML with the new name
- Triggers a compose build via triggerAndPollComposeJob
- Updates the default agent reference via PUT /api/scopes/default-agent
- Shows loading spinner on Save button during the async operation
- Displays toast notifications for success/error

Closes #4185

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(platform): improve zero skills ux across onboarding and meet page

- fix skill selection after OAuth/API-token connect by calling toggleSkill
  in async chain instead of broken render-time detection
- fix ConnectModal OAuth flow not selecting skill (onSuccess now fires
  after connect resolves)
- add skeleton loading to meet page skills list during data fetch
- remove auto-disconnect on skill removal in meet page
- use useLastLoadable for connector types to prevent list refresh flicker
- render onboarding skill cards immediately (sync data) without skeleton gate
- fix Clerk session touch causing onboarding dialog unmount (useLastLoadable)
- fix React "Cannot update during render" warnings in app shell and skills

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(platform): sync meet skills ui and enhance add skill dialog

- Update skills list to single-card layout with border-separated rows
- Replace X button with 3-dot dropdown menu for remove/disconnect
- Use ConnectorIcon consistently across skills list and add skill dialog
- Replace custom add skill dialog with AddConnectionDialog (variant="zero")
- Card click: connected skills add directly, unconnected trigger connect
- OAuth+API key connectors show only API key button in card
- Only add skill on successful connect (connectConnector$ returns status)
- Add fuzzy search to add skill dialog with fixed 85vh height
- Add toast notifications for skill add/remove
- Update labels: "Super Manager" to "Super agent" with crown icon
- Remove unused addConnectionDialogTab$ signals

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings for zero-meet signals and settings dialog

- fix zeroCompose$ to throw on failed fetch instead of silently returning null
- add integration tests for zero-meet signals (addZeroSkill$, removeZeroSkill$,
  zeroUpdateSettings$, zeroAddedSkills$) with optimistic update and rollback coverage
- restore settings-page AddConnectionDialog tabbed UI (Connectors + Custom API)
  that was broken by zero variant refactoring
- restore addConnectionDialogTab$ signal removed during refactoring

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: fail fast on missing compose and extract shared build helper

- make zeroUpdateSettings$ throw when compose content is missing instead
  of silently returning (fail-fast principle)
- extract buildAndSetDefaultAgent helper to deduplicate compose-job +
  default-agent update logic between syncSkillsToCompose$ and
  zeroUpdateSettings$

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: only toggle skill on successful connect, not on modal dismiss

The onboarding ConnectModal was calling toggleSkill in onClose, which
fires even when the user dismisses without connecting. Move the toggle
to onSuccess so skills are only selected after a successful connection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use scope resolution for compose access control instead of scope_members

canAccessCompose was checking the local scope_members table for org
membership, which can be out of sync with Clerk (the source of truth).
This caused 404s when a user's JWT orgId differed from their actual
org membership discovered via Clerk API.

Replace the scope_members DB check with resolveScope() which uses
JWT + Clerk API fallback, matching how resolveScope already works
elsewhere. Also reload onboarding status after updating default agent
so the frontend picks up the new compose ID.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add org_cache table and cache service for non-jwt contexts (#4239)

* feat: add org_cache table and cache service for non-jwt contexts

Add a DB-backed cache for Clerk org data (slug, tier) keyed by
clerkOrgId, with a 1-minute TTL aligned with JWT token TTL.

- Drizzle schema for org_cache with TEXT primary key
- getOrgData(clerkOrgId) with cache-miss → Clerk API → upsert flow
- Integration tests for cache hit, miss, stale, and publicMetadata tier
- Extend clerk-mock with getOrganization mock
- Add org_cache test helpers to api-test-helpers

Closes #4231

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct migration journal timestamp ordering for 0124

The migration 0124_dark_quasimodo had a timestamp earlier than
0123_rare_longshot, which violates Drizzle's requirement for strictly
increasing timestamps and would cause the migration to be skipped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add fail-fast slug validation and runtime tier type check in org-cache-service

- Throw error when Clerk org has no slug instead of silently falling back to clerkOrgId
- Add runtime typeof check for tier from publicMetadata instead of unsafe type assertion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: support org query param as alternative to scope slug (#4237)

* feat: support org query param as alternative to scope slug

Add ?org=<clerkOrgId> as an alternative query parameter alongside
?scope=<slug> for scope resolution. Both work simultaneously during
the Clerk consumer migration. Clients can gradually migrate to ?org=.

Changes:
- Update requireScopeFromRequest() to accept ?org= as alternative
- Add org: z.string().optional() to 3 ts-rest contracts
- Update 20 API route files to extract and pass ?org= param
- Add integration tests for ?org= resolution and priority

Closes #4232

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: apply authorization check for org query param path

The canAccessCompose check was only applied when ?scope= was used for
cross-scope lookups, but not when ?org= was used. This could allow
users to bypass access control by switching from ?scope= to ?org=.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add integration tests for org param authorization in composes route

Add tests verifying that canAccessCompose check is applied when using
?org= for cross-scope lookups, covering both permitted and denied cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: update error message assertions for scope/org param validation

Update 4 test files that asserted on the old error message
"scope query parameter is required" to match the new message
"scope or org query parameter is required".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci: extract ansible provisioning into reusable composite action (#4277)

Move inline ansible provisioning from crates.yml and turbo.yml into a
dedicated composite action (.github/actions/provision). Each playbook
(runner and monitoring) is checked independently via git blob hash, and
only stale hosts are provisioned. Reuses the caller's SSH tunnel and
runner VM instead of spinning up a separate job.

Closes #4276

readme_html:
Documentation / Website / Discord

VM0 runs natural language-described workflows automatically on schedule in remote sandbox environments.

⭐ Star us on GitHub, it motivates us a lot! ⭐

🔥 What you GET

  • Just Claude Code, zero abstraction, nothing new to learn
  • Cloud sandbox, run Claude Code in isolated claude sandbox 24/7
  • Skill Native, Compatible with 35,738+ skills in skills.sh, and 70+ high quality SaaS integration skill like GitHub, Slack, Notion, Firecrawl, and more,
  • Persistence, continue chat, resume, fork, and version your workflow sessions
  • Observability, logs, metrics, and network visibility for every run

🚀 Quick Start

From zero to workflow agent in 5 minutes

npm install -g @vm0/cli && vm0 onboard

📕 Showcase

Onboard

Agent Build

📚 Architecture

  • Architecture Documentation - Comprehensive technical reference covering sandbox technologies (Firecracker microVMs), infrastructure components and network architecture

For user-facing guides and tutorials, visit docs.vm0.ai.

⭐ Star History
